document_title: LLM Harness Engineering: Market and Breakpoint Analysis

reference.notes: Selected example is a VC market-framing piece (b2venture) useful for taxonomy and wedge thinking but must be supplemented with primary-source evidence and traction data.

sections.title: Executive summary
sections.summary: Concise summary of definition, state, top providers, gaps, top 3 wedges, recommended wedge.

sections.title: Market map and taxonomy
sections.summary: Define LLM harness engineering and present a structured taxonomy with evidence and boundaries.

sections.title: Provider landscape
sections.summary: Detailed, evidence-backed provider analysis organized by category (commercial and open-source). Section includes per-provider citations and synthesis; remaining appendix population noted in section file.

sections.title: Customer and JTBD analysis
sections.summary: User personas, jobs-to-be-done, buying criteria, and frustrations.
sections.evidence_plan: Consolidated public-source evidence added to section; outstanding: conduct 6–10 interviews across personas; add 2–3 independent engineering case studies/postmortems; run an integration spike (LangChain+LlamaIndex) and publish effort-sizing; populate appendix with per-row source links for persona table and provider comparison matrix.

sections.title: Technical bottlenecks
sections.summary: Analyze solved, partially solved, and unsolved technical problems.

sections.title: Commercial landscape and moat analysis
sections.summary: Where value accrues, defensibility assessment, role of open source.

sections.title: Breakpoint analysis and ranked wedges
sections.summary: Ranked newcomer wedges with evidence and risk assessment.

sections.title: Strategic recommendations
sections.summary: Product recommendation, GTM, roadmap, metrics for PMF.

sections.title: Appendix: sources and comparison matrices
sections.summary: Source list, company table, comparison matrix, terminology notes.
